---
title: "prexorctl diagnostics bundle"
description: "Collect a redacted diagnostics bundle for support / postmortems"
---

<!-- auto-generated by tools/gen-cli-docs.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

Collect a redacted diagnostics bundle for support / postmortems.
The bundle includes:
  manifest.json    - bundle metadata + controller version
  readiness.json   - readiness probe snapshot
  overview.json    - cluster counts (nodes, instances, players, groups)
  settings.json    - non-sensitive runtime settings
  config.json      - controller config with secrets redacted
  redis.json       - Redis-protocol keyspace summary
  leases.json      - distributed lease holders
  logs.txt         - recent controller log records (best-effort)
Sensitive fields (JWT secrets, admin password, URI credentials) are redacted
server-side before transport. Logs older than the controller log buffer
capacity are not retrievable through this surface; rotate the bundle close
to the incident or scrape on-disk logs separately.

## Usage

```text
prexorctl diagnostics bundle [flags]
```

## Flags

```text
--burn-after-read   Destroy the paste on first read
        --expiry string     Paste expiry preset: 1h | 1d | 30d | never
    -h, --help              help for bundle
        --log-lines int     Number of recent log lines to include (0 to skip) (default 500)
    -o, --out string        Output path (default: ./prexorctl-diag-<timestamp>.tar.gz)
        --public            Mark the paste public (overrides share.defaultPrivate=true)
        --share             Upload a redacted copy to the configured paste service and print the link
```

## Global flags

```text
--ascii               Use ASCII glyphs only (no unicode box drawing, sparklines, etc.)
        --context string      Override active context for this invocation
    -c, --controller string   Override controller URL
    -j, --json                Output in JSON format
        --no-color            Disable colored output
    -t, --token string        Override auth token
    -v, --verbose             Show HTTP request/response details
```
